verses.latin_vulgate: Exaudi, Deus, orationem meam cum deprecor ;  a timore inimici eripe animam meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁמַע־אֱלֹהִ֣ים קוֹלִ֣י בְשִׂיחִ֑י מִפַּ֥חַד א֝וֹיֵ֗ב תִּצֹּ֥ר חַיָּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Hear O God, my prayer, when I make supplication to thee: deliver my soul from the fear of the enemy.
verses.finnish_biblia: Davidin Psalmi, edelläveisaajalle. (H64:2) Kuule, Jumala, ääntäni, minun valituksessani: varjele elämäni vihollisen pelvosta.
verses.king_james: Hear my voice, O God, in my prayer: preserve my life from fear of the enemy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Protexisti me a conventu malignantium,  a multitudine operantium iniquitatem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּ֭סְתִּירֵנִי מִסּ֣וֹד מְרֵעִ֑ים מֵ֝רִגְשַׁ֗ת פֹּ֣עֲלֵי אָֽוֶן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thou hast protected me from the assembly of the malignant; from the multitude of the workers of iniquity.
verses.finnish_biblia: Peitä minua pahain neuvosta, väärintekiäin metelistä,
verses.king_james: Hide me from the secret counsel of the wicked; from the insurrection of the workers of iniquity:

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia exacuerunt ut gladium linguas suas ;  intenderunt arcum rem amaram,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֤ר שָׁנְנ֣וּ כַחֶ֣רֶב לְשׁוֹנָ֑ם דָּרְכ֥וּ חִ֝צָּ֗ם דָּבָ֥ר מָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: For they have whetted their tongues like a sword; they have bent their bow a bitter thing,
verses.finnish_biblia: Jotka kielensä hiovat niinkuin miekan: he ampuvat myrkyllisillä sanoillansa niinkuin nuolilla;
verses.king_james: Who whet their tongue like a sword, and bend their bows to shoot their arrows, even bitter words:

verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִירֹ֣ת בַּמִּסְתָּרִ֣ים תָּ֑ם פִּתְאֹ֥ם יֹ֝רֻ֗הוּ וְלֹ֣א יִירָֽאוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: To shoot in secret the undefiled.
verses.finnish_biblia: Että he salaisesti ampuisivat viatointa: he ampuvat häntä äkisti ilman pelvota.
verses.king_james: That they may shoot in secret at the perfect: suddenly do they shoot at him, and fear not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Subito sagittabunt eum, et non timebunt ;  firmaverunt sibi sermonem nequam.  Narraverunt ut absconderent laqueos ;  dixerunt : Quis videbit eos ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְחַזְּקוּ־לָ֨מוֹ ׀ דָּ֘בָ֤ר רָ֗ע יְֽ֭סַפְּרוּ לִטְמ֣וֹן מוֹקְשִׁ֑ים אָ֝מְר֗וּ מִ֣י יִרְאֶה־לָּֽמוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: They will shoot at him on a sudden, and will not fear: they are resolute in wickedness. They have talked of hiding snares; they have said: Who shall see them?
verses.finnish_biblia: He vahvistavat itsensä pahoissa juonissansa ja puhuvat, kuinka he paulat virittäisivät, ja sanovat: kuka taitaa ne nähdä?
verses.king_james: They encourage themselves in an evil matter: they commune of laying snares privily; they say, Who shall see them?

verses.latin_vulgate: Scrutati sunt iniquitates ;  defecerunt scrutantes scrutinio.  Accedet homo ad cor altum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַ֥חְפְּֽשׂוּ־עוֹלֹ֗ת תַּ֭מְנוּ חֵ֣פֶשׂ מְחֻפָּ֑שׂ וְקֶ֥רֶב אִ֝֗ישׁ וְלֵ֣ב עָמֹֽק׃
verses.douay_rheims: They have searched after iniquities: they have failed in their search. Man shall come to a deep heart:
verses.finnish_biblia: He ajattelevat vääryyttä, ja täyttävät sen minkä he ajatelleet ovat; viekkaat ihmiset ja salakavalat sydämet.
verses.king_james: They search out iniquities; they accomplish a diligent search: both the inward thought of every one of them, and the heart, is deep.

verses.latin_vulgate: et exaltabitur Deus.  Sagittæ parvulorum factæ sunt plagæ eorum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּרֵ֗ם אֱלֹ֫הִ֥ים חֵ֥ץ פִּתְא֑וֹם הָ֝י֗וּ מַכּוֹתָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And God shall be exalted. The arrows of children are their wounds:
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Jumala ampuu heitä; äkillinen nuoli on heidän rangaistuksensa.
verses.king_james: But God shall shoot at them with an arrow; suddenly shall they be wounded.

verses.latin_vulgate: et infirmatæ sunt contra eos linguæ eorum.  Conturbati sunt omnes qui videbant eos,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּכְשִׁיל֣וּהוּ עָלֵ֣ימוֹ לְשׁוֹנָ֑ם יִ֝תְנֹדְד֗וּ כׇּל־רֹ֥אֵה בָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And their tongues against them are made weak. All that saw them were troubled;
verses.finnish_biblia: Heidän oma kielensä pitää heitä langettaman, että jokainen, joka heidät näkee, pakenee heitä.
verses.king_james: So they shall make their own tongue to fall upon themselves: all that see them shall flee away.

verses.latin_vulgate: et timuit omnis homo.  Et annuntiaverunt opera Dei,  et facta ejus intellexerunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּֽירְא֗וּ כׇּל־אָ֫דָ֥ם וַ֭יַּגִּידוּ פֹּ֥עַל אֱלֹהִ֗ים וּֽמַעֲשֵׂ֥הוּ הִשְׂכִּֽילוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And every man was afraid. And they declared the works of God, and understood his doings.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kaikkein ihmisten pitää pelkäämän, ja Jumalan työtä ilmoittaman, ja hänen työnsä ymmärtämän.
verses.king_james: And all men shall fear, and shall declare the work of God; for they shall wisely consider of his doing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Lætabitur justus in Domino, et sperabit in eo,  et laudabuntur omnes recti corde.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִשְׂמַ֬ח צַדִּ֣יק בַּ֭יהֹוָה וְחָ֣סָה ב֑וֹ וְ֝יִתְהַלְל֗וּ כׇּל־יִשְׁרֵי־לֵֽב׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: The just shall rejoice in the Lord, and shall hope in him: and all the upright in heart shall be praised.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vanhurskaat pitää Herrassa riemuitseman, ja häneen uskaltaman; ja kaikki vaat sydämet kerskatkoon siitä.
verses.king_james: The righteous shall be glad in the Lord, and shall trust in him; and all the upright in heart shall glory.
